value: 일본 국외 거주 외국인 여권 소지자. 'Temporary Visitor' 체류자격으로 입국한 자만 구매 가능. 일본 국적자 또는 일본 거주 외국인은 구매 불가.

value.비고: 2023년 10월 인상 후 유지. 2025-2026 추가 인상 없음.

value: 한국 국적자는 90일 이내 관광 목적 무비자 입국 가능. 여권 잔여 유효기간 제한 없음 (체류 기간 이상 권장). Visit Japan Web 사전 등록 권장.
Suica/PASMO 등 IC카드로 전철, 버스, 편의점 결제 가능. 물리 카드 재고 부족은 2025.3월 공식 해소. Welcome Suica(관광객용, 28일 유효) 주요 역에서 판매 재개. 모바일 Suica(Apple/Google) 및 Welcome Suica Mobile(iPhone, 180일 유효) 이용 가능.
지정석(Reserved)과 자유석(Non-reserved) 구분. JR Pass 소지 시 지정석 무료 예약 가능 (Nozomi/Mizuho 제외). 자유석은 선착순 탑승.
면세 범위: 주류 3병(760ml 이하), 담배 200개비(거주자)/400개비(비거주자), 향수 2oz, 기타 품목 20만엔 이하. 초과 시 신고 및 관세 부과.

value.비고: 구급차 무료, 치료비는 별도. 119/110은 일본어 우선이나 통역 연결 가능.

value: Visit Japan Web은 웹 기반 서비스(앱 불필요). 여권 정보, 입국심사(하선카드), 세관신고, 면세쇼핑 3개 항목 사전 등록. 등록 후 QR코드 발급 → 공항 도착 시 제시. 2026년 현재 필수는 아니나 강력 권장 (심사 시간 대폭 단축). 스크린샷 불가, 라이브 접속 필요.

신제도_2026_11월.방식: 환급형 (구매 시 세금 포함 → 출국 시 환급)

value: Collected info for eligibility from Headed to Japan? Save these emergency contacts and places to ...

Citizens from most European countries, United States, Australia, and Argentina do not need a Japan Tourist Visa for tourism purposes as they are visa-exempt and can stay up to 90 days with Temporary Visitor status.
Foreigners staying in Japan can use Visit Japan Web for immigration procedures and tax-free purchases during their stay.

Tourists who are foreign visitors staying in Japan for less than 6 months are eligible for tax exemption on articles purchased at specified tax-free shops, provided the articles are taken out of Japan.
Average price for a private room in a hostel or guesthouse in Japan is $32 per night
Hostel prices in Tokyo are cheapest in March with average rates of $31/night
Hostels in Tokyo in Shibuya neighborhood have an average price of $102 per night
1,000 yen for local residents, 2,500 yen for foreign tourists
500 JPY/adult | 250 JPY/student and senior from 65 years old | Free for child 15 years old and under
1,500 JPY/adult | 700 JPY/child 04-12 years old
1,000 JPY/adult | 500 JPY/school child | 200 JPY/child aged 03 years old
800 JPY/adult | 400 JPY/child 04-15 years old | Free for child under 04
The Japan Rail Pass is available only to non-Japanese nationals visiting Japan on a tourist visa (Temporary Visitor status).
Filling set meals around 1000 yen; lunch boxes around 500 yen
If you purchase a pass to register with your Welcome Suica card, you must pay for the pass at the same time as purchasing the card.
nearby Kyoto Station or in downtown Kyoto
Collected info for tips from What to Expect When Staying at a Traditional Ryokan in Japan

In Japanese apartments, it is important to be conscious of how sound travels between neighboring and upstairs/downstairs units; noise etiquette is essential to maintain peaceful living and good relationships with neighbors.

To avoid crowds at major tourist sights in Japan, go early in the day, using your jet lag to your advantage.

Travelers can purchase a Japan SIM card either after arriving in Japan or before traveling. Providers such as NINJA WiFi allow travelers to order a Japan SIM card online in advance and easily pick it up upon arrival.
Sparks eSIM offers unlimited data with partnerships with leading Japanese carriers, ensuring priority access to high-speed networks and seamless network transitions during high-speed rail travel.
Typical dinner time in Japan generally falls between 6:00 p.m. and 8:00 p.m.

Prepaid SIM cards can be purchased at some convenience stores and electronics shops in Japan for travelers who did not buy a SIM card at the airport. Ordering a SIM card online before arriving in Japan is also an option.
Bulgari Hotel Tokyo

Kaiseki is Japan’s most refined dining, almost synonymous with Western haute cuisine, requiring the highest level of etiquette and respect for the food, typically prepared by experienced professional chefs.
The validity period of a Japan Rail Pass is the consecutive 7, 14, or 21 days beginning on the date the pass can actually be first used.
Japan is still a predominantly cash-based country, with cash payments making up 82% of all payments in 2018.
A Suica card will be made invalid after 10 years of inactivity
IC cards can be used to pay for fares on trains, subways, buses, and ferries in Japan.
The Shinkansen, or bullet train, made its debut in 1964, connecting Tokyo and Osaka in about four hours, revolutionizing travel with high speed and dependability.
A taxi ride from Haneda Airport to Tokyo takes around 30-40 minutes
JR Kansai Airport Rapid Service takes about 50 minutes to Osaka Station
Airport shuttle bus travel time is around 25 minutes to central Osaka
Narita Express train takes 53 minutes to Tokyo Station
Limousine buses to Osaka Station take 30 minutes
JR Rapid Airport train from New Chitose Airport to Sapporo takes 37 minutes
Train journey from Narita Airport to Tokyo takes about 1 hour
Many restaurants in Japan close around 2 PM

To connect to Wi-Fi at major international airports in Japan, you can use free Wi-Fi services available within terminals such as Narita, Haneda, Kansai, New Chitose, Centrair, and Fukuoka airports. Connection methods differ by airport, so travelers should check specific airport Wi-Fi instructions.
Japan is a cash society, but credit cards and debit cards are accepted in most places in Japanese cities.

Cashless payments in Japan include credit cards, QR/barcode payments (mobile payments), electronic money, and debit cards. Popular QR code payment apps are PayPay, LinePay, Rpay (Rakuten Pay), and MerPay. Credit cards such as Visa, JCB, and Mastercard are widely accepted. IC travel cards can be purchased at station ticket counters or vending machines and used for payments at convenience stores, shops, and vending machines; some IC travel cards require a deposit, but tourist IC travel cards may not require one.

Eating like a local in Tokyo involves being adventurous, stepping outside usual tourist zones, trying counter dining, following locals, and exploring small local restaurants even if there is no English menu or staff speaks little English.
SIM cards can be purchased at airports in Japan for immediate activation upon arrival.
Residents with bank accounts in Japan can apply for a debit card, which may require selecting payment options for online shopping when setting up the card.

Use Sparks eSIM with unlimited data for seamless access to international and local communication platforms, benefiting from partnerships with leading Japanese carriers that ensure priority access to high-speed networks and seamless network transitions during high-speed rail travel.
Saying “itadakimasu” before a meal is a way of expressing gratitude in Japanese dining etiquette.
Most IC cards require a security deposit of 500 yen when purchasing the card

The yen exchange rate currently ensures that Japan remains great value on the ground, with incredible restaurants and impressive sights all very reasonably priced and surprisingly cheap compared to many countries in the West.
Japan allows visa-free entry for short stays ranging from 15 to 90 days for tourism purposes for many nationalities including British, EU, USA, Canada, and Australia.
For countries that are not visa-exempt, travelers can apply online for a Japan tourist visa for stays up to 90 days using the JAPAN eVISA platform.
Visa applications and inquiries at the Embassy of Japan in Washington DC are accepted only from residents of Washington DC, Maryland, and Virginia.
Welcome Suica is currently the only card-type IC card available for travelers to purchase at designated locations in Japan.

From November 1, 2026, Japan’s tax-free shopping system will change to a refund-based system where visitors pay the consumption tax at purchase and receive a refund after completing procedures before departure.
